query: What two conditions must a problem meet to be classified as NP-Complete?
expected_answer: It must be in class NP (verifiable in polynomial time) and it must be NP-Hard (all other NP problems can be reduced to it in polynomial time).

query: How does the Bellman-Ford algorithm detect a negative-weight cycle?
expected_answer: By relaxing all edges V-1 times, and then doing one more pass. If any distance decreases in the final pass, a negative cycle exists.

query: Compare the time complexity of Rabin-Karp with KMP when matching a pattern.
expected_answer: Rabin-Karp has an average complexity of O(n+m) but worst-case O(nm) due to hash collisions. KMP guarantees O(n+m) worst-case time.

query: What advantage does Bellman-Ford have over Dijkstra's algorithm regarding graph structures?
expected_answer: Bellman-Ford can handle graphs with negative edge weights, whereas Dijkstra's algorithm fails if negative weights are present.

expected_answer: There is no general sorting algorithm that operates in O(1) time.
